overview: This Pioneer deck mills cards into the graveyard to reduce the cost of Delve creatures, then uses Neoform or Eldritch Evolution to cheat Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play. Atraxa provides massive card advantage and enables a recurring loop, while Soulflayer serves as a resilient backup threat that gains keywords from sacrificed Atraxas. The deck prioritizes deep deck-diving tools over traditional cantrips to accelerate the combo and protect it with targeted disruption.
win_conditions: Casting Atraxa, Grand Unifier via Neoform or Eldritch Evolution to draw cards and establish a board presence
Sacrificing Atraxa to Soulflayer to create a 4/4 with Flying, Deathtouch, Vigilance, and Lifelink
Using Tyrranax Rex as a resilient clock against targeted removal

keep_criteria: Hands containing a first-turn disruption spell, a second-turn mill spell, and combo pieces for turn three
Hands with combo pieces, disruption, and two lands that can find the third land and a Delve spell via Otherworldly Gaze or Contingency Plan
mulligan_criteria: Any one-lander hand due to the deck's mana greedy nature
Hands requiring two perfect turns to act when facing aggressive decks
Hands lacking a first-turn option or protection against opponent's Thoughtseize

examples.reason: Allows casting Thoughtseize on turn one, mill on turn two, and perform the combo on turn three.
Can cast Soulflayer on turn three with Denial as backup, then sacrifice it with Evolution on turn four, though slower.
One-landers are strongly recommended to mulligan because the deck needs consistent land drops to execute the mana-intensive combo.

early_game: Prioritize land drops to avoid mana screw in a greedy mana base
Cast Thoughtseize to remove opponent disruption and gauge their hand
Begin milling cards with Otherworldly Gaze or Contingency Plan to enable Delve
mid_game: Cast Delve creatures cheaply once enough cards are in the graveyard
Use Neoform or Eldritch Evolution to cheat Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play
Protect threats with Stubborn Denial once a creature is on the battlefield
late_game: Use Atraxa's card draw to find more combo pieces or mill spells
Sacrifice Atraxa to Soulflayer for a resilient, keyword-heavy threat
Apply pressure with Tyrranax Rex if Atraxa is removed or the combo stalls

key_cards.name: Atraxa, Grand Unifier
key_cards.role: Main win condition / Card draw engine
key_cards.notes: Cheated into play via Neoform/Evolution; draws 3-4 cards; feeds Soulflayer for keywords.

key_cards.name: Neoform
key_cards.notes: Fetches Atraxa from graveyard; requires mill setup to reduce cost.

key_cards.name: Soulflayer
key_cards.notes: Gains keywords from sacrificed Atraxa; provides a fair Plan B and five-turn clock.

key_cards.name: Otherworldly Gaze
key_cards.notes: Puts cards into graveyard to reduce Delve costs; helps find lands and pieces.

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Removes opponent's interaction; helps time the combo safely.

key_cards.name: Stubborn Denial
key_cards.notes: Functions as Negate if a creature is on the battlefield; protects Atraxa or threats.

key_cards.name: Tyrranax Rex
key_cards.notes: Hard to kill with targeted removal; pressures control/midrange decks.

key_cards.name: Contingency Plan
key_cards.notes: Finds Tasigur or Soulflayer; mills to enable Delve.

key_cards.name: Eldritch Evolution
key_cards.notes: Alternative to Neoform for fetching Atraxa.

key_cards.name: Mana Confluence
key_cards.notes: Four copies included for color consistency; replaces utility lands.

sequencing_tips: Cast Thoughtseize on turn one to remove disruption, then mill on turn two, and combo on turn three.
Reverse spell order if needed to ensure Neoform goes unimpeded, but risk opponent's Thoughtseize.
Use Stubborn Denial to protect Atraxa or other threats once a creature is on the battlefield.
If Contingency Plan finds nothing, hold Soulflayer for turn three and sacrifice it with Eldritch Evolution on turn four.

matchups.advice: Pressure with Tyrranax Rex; sideboard in Leyline of Sanctity and Assassin's Trophy; remove Atraxa, Soulflayer, and Stubborn Denial.
Close the game quickly before opponent's interaction resolves; reduce consistency slightly to handle hate.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: put enough cards into the graveyard to cast a creature with Delve on the second or third turn, then cast Neoform and thus look for Atraxa, Grand Unifier

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: cast Neoform and thus look for Atraxa, Grand Unifier which... will also guarantee an average of four more cards
Soulflayer wants to feed on because it grants four relevant keywords to our creature
Tyrranax Rex as an efficient answer against archetypes that rely on targeted removals

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: cast Thoughtseize on the first turn, Otherworldly Gaze on the second, and perform the combo on the third
reverse the spell order in this situation to ensure a safer opportunity to play Neoform unimpeded
Stubborn Denial, which works as a Negate if any of our creatures are in play
casting Soulflayer on the third turn with Stubborn Denial as a backup to sacrifice it with Eldritch Evolution on the fourth

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Thoughtseize is one of our biggest enemies in the format
dealing with more resolved hates like Rest in Peace, Grafdigger's Cage and Unlicensed Hearse
archetypes that rely on targeted removals to deal with our creatures
Dispel is an efficient answer to Instant-Speed removals and counterspells
